APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Daniel Martinez de la Vega Esq. (415-287-6203; dvega@vegalawyer.com) for Plaintiff.
Peter Van Zandt Esq. (415-362-2580; Peter.VanZandt@lewisbrisbois.com) of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ULYSSES RAMIREZ' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions:

Motion GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Rog 28 denied: the response that there are no other documents is Code compliant. Rogs 30-31, 33-34, 36, 38-40, 45-46 denied: Lyft permissibly exercised its right to identify documents, CCP 2030.230. Rog 32, 35, 37 granted: the record does not establish that Lyft's production identified the person who took the identified action. Rog 41, 49, 65, 89, 93 granted in part. Lyft's reference to documents is nonresponsive, but the Court cannot compel identification of persons who performed investigations for Lyft if it does not perform investigations. If Lyft cannot answer the interrogatory as phrased, it should say so. Rog 53 denied - the response is complete and Code compliant. RFP 79 denied. First, the request for all documents "relating to" contacts is overbroad because it is unclear how such a request is limited. Second, Plaintiff's objection seeking documents that show the rules, policies, scope of work, or other guidelines followed by Lyft's Trust and Safety is not what was requested. Plaintiff may rephrase the request along these more narrow lines. RFP 80-81 granted. These requests are discoverable and the offer to permit Lyft to redact is sufficient. Sanctions DENIED because the motion is granted in part and denied in part.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: A. Datt, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
